response:
<<Flight Time Optimization and Exponential Thrills>>

Profit The executive has a total flight duration of 8 hours and intends to spend 2 hours solving a math problem, leaving exactly 6 hours available for reading.
Profit With 480 pages to read within this remaining 6-hour window, the executive must increase their reading speed to a minimum of 80 pages per hour to finish the book.
Profit The excitement level of the thriller, starting at 100 units and growing at 5% per hour, can be modeled by the exponential equation $E(t) = 100(1.05)^t$, where $t$ represents the time in hours.
Profit After the full 8-hour flight, the calculated excitement level reaches approximately 147.75 units when substituting $t = 8$ into the formulated equation.
